ReleaseLabel: Extension 0.30.0 (Sets 061 'Explorer UX Polish' + 062 'Lightweight Verification Affordance') + ai_router 0.17.0
Notes: Combined ad-hoc per-set UAT gating BOTH releases (the 059->060 fold precedent): extension 0.30.0 carries Set 061's deferred surfaces plus Set 062's, and ai_router 0.17.0 carries the blessed verification-mode writer. This checklist SUBSUMES docs/session-sets/061-explorer-ux-polish/061-explorer-ux-polish-uat-checklist.json. UAT runs against a LOCAL build (.vsix or F5 dev host), never a published one (Set 058 lesson). DESIGNED FOR A SHORT OPERATOR PASS: every mechanically-coverable row was pre-verified by the AI orchestrator on 2026-06-12 (Passes=true / Result=complete, the deterministic check named in ProgrammaticVerification) against this suite baseline: Python 1216 passed + 1 skipped; TS unit 864 passing with exactly the 2 tracked pre-existing Set-026 failures (configEditor-foundation, notificationsSection) -- the agreed-green baseline; drift guards clean. The operator walk is the 'Operator walk' items only, in order, ~15 minutes, all against the disposable fixture workspace from `npm run make-uat-workspace` (the generated copy pins dabblerSessionSets.pythonPath to the repo .venv, so the python-backed row actions work with zero setup; mutating actions are safe -- the copy is throwaway). If the Explorer does not auto-refresh after a mutating action, use the view's refresh button. Row-state references (001..006) are the fixture sets documented in test-fixtures/uat-matrix/README.md.

Review.ItemLabel: All six hello-world-lightweight rows render their expected marker/fraction states on one screen
Review.HumanAction: Open the generated uat-matrix workspace and look at the hello-world-lightweight rows in the Session Set Explorer. Compare each against: 001-greet-the-world = 'lw' only (no v-marker, no '+'); 002-greet-quietly = 'lw' + 'v?'; 003-greet-with-note = 'lw' only (note suppresses the marker); 004-add-a-farewell = 'lw' + fraction '1/2+' (no 'v+'); 005-shout-the-greeting = 'lw' + '2/2+' AND 'v+'; 006-whisper-mode = 'lw' + plain '3/3' (no marker, no '+').
Review.Expectation: Every row matches the table. No positive 'verified' badge anywhere — quiet is success on 003 and 006.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: uatMatrixFixtures.test.ts (14 tests) derives every row through the real readSessionSets scan and pins these exact signals — but it runs on a vscode-stub, so the live webview rendering is what this item confirms (the Set 058 lesson).
Review.Feedback: You accidentally repeated some numbers, but what you have shown matches your the table.

Review.ItemLabel: All four hello-world-full rows render: clean control, two blocked markers, one needs-migration asterisk
Review.HumanAction: Look at the hello-world-full rows: 001-hello-page = fraction '1/3' with the in-flight annotation and NO markers (no 'lw', no 'v?'/'v+', no '+'); 002-style-the-greeting = blocked chain marker (⛓︎); 003-publish-the-page = blocked chain marker (⛓︎); 004-legacy-greeting-notes = asterisk (*) needs-migration marker.
Review.Expectation: Control row is completely unmarked (Full tier is the quiet default). Both blocked rows show the chain glyph; the old all-caps '[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]' badge is absent. The v3 row shows the asterisk.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: uatMatrixFixtures.test.ts pins the derived signals; prerequisites.test.ts pins blocked derivation incl. unknown-slug; live rendering is the operator portion.

Review.ItemLabel: Hover copy on v?, v+, '+' fraction, verdict-enriched fraction, and lw
Review.HumanAction: Hover, in order: (a) 'v?' on 002-greet-quietly; (b) 'v+' on 005-shout-the-greeting; (c) the '2/2+' fraction on 005; (d) the '3/3' fraction on 006-whisper-mode; (e) the 'lw' marker on any lightweight row.
Review.Expectation: (a) 'Lightweight — verification is out-of-band or none. The Explorer cannot tell whether this set was reviewed out of band. Click for verification options.' (b) 'Dedicated verification enabled — a verification/remediation session is still owed or in flight. Click for the next step.' (c) 'Lightweight dedicated-sessions set: verification/remediation sessions are appended when the work sessions complete, so the session count can still grow.' (d) contains 'Verification: VERIFIED (session 3)'. (e) 'Lightweight tier — router-off; verification per the set's verificationMode.'
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: verificationMarker.test.ts + tierLegibility.test.ts pin these strings as constants; live tooltip surfacing is the operator portion.

Review.ItemLabel: Blocked tooltips name the prerequisite and its state (incl. unknown slug); asterisk names the on-disk schema
Review.HumanAction: Hover: (a) the chain marker on hello-world-full/002-style-the-greeting; (b) the chain marker on 003-publish-the-page; (c) the asterisk on 004-legacy-greeting-notes.
Review.Expectation: (a) names '001-hello-page (in progress)' in the 'Blocked by prerequisites: … — all must complete first.' format. (b) names '099-cdn-rollout (unknown set — check the slug)'. (c) says the set ran under schema v3.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: prerequisites.test.ts pins the tooltip format incl. the unknown-slug arm; uatMatrixFixtures.test.ts pins schemaVersionOnDisk=3 for row 004.

Review.ItemLabel: All markers (lw, v?, v+, ⛓︎, *) read as quiet, de-emphasized annotations
Review.HumanAction: Glance over both project folders' rows.
Review.Expectation: Markers are visually muted (lower opacity / muted color, help cursor on hover) and do not compete with the set names.

Review.ItemLabel: Clicking v? / v+ opens the row context menu and never mutates state
Review.HumanAction: Click the 'v?' marker on 002-greet-quietly; observe the QuickPick that opens (note it offers 'Open External Verification Note' and 'Set Up Dedicated Verification…'); press Esc. Click the 'v+' marker on 005-shout-the-greeting; observe it offers 'Verification Kickoff'; press Esc.
Review.Expectation: Each click opens the row's context QuickPick. Dismissing changes nothing — rows re-render identically.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Marker click posts the existing showRowContextMenu message (verificationMarker.test.ts + webview protocol tests); no mutation path exists in the handler.

Review.ItemLabel: 'Verification Kickoff' copies a pointer-style, typed-session, different-engine handoff prompt
Review.HumanAction: On 005-shout-the-greeting, open the row menu and pick 'Verification Kickoff'. Paste the clipboard into any editor tab and read it.
Review.Expectation: The prompt references (not embeds): the 'Mode B — dedicated-sessions' part of Step 6 in docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md; checking the per-session orchestrator blocks to confirm a DIFFERENT engine; opening the typed session via `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir … --type verification`; and chaining remediation via `--type remediation --handoff`. No doc bodies are pasted in; the set's spec/activity-log/state file paths are listed.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: copyPromptCommands tests pin the builder content (references, sanitization, no embedded bodies); actionRegistry tests pin eligibility (Lightweight Mode-B, no completed verification).

Review.Subarea: Operator walk 8 — external note clears v?
Review.ItemLabel: 'Open External Verification Note' creates external-verification.md and the v? marker clears
Review.HumanAction: On 002-greet-quietly, open the row menu and pick 'Open External Verification Note'. The file opens (created empty). Return to the Explorer (refresh if needed).
Review.Expectation: 002's 'v?' marker is gone — the row now reads like 003-greet-with-note (quiet is success). No other change to the row.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: verificationMarker.test.ts pins note-presence suppression; externalVerification.ts creates with flag 'wx' (never truncates an existing note).

Review.FunctionalArea: Sanctioned A→B on completed sets (062 D3/D4)
Review.ItemLabel: 'Set Up Dedicated Verification…' on a COMPLETED Mode-A set records the transition through the blessed writer
Review.HumanAction: Still on 002-greet-quietly (complete, Mode A), open the row menu and pick 'Set Up Dedicated Verification…'. Confirm through the one-way confirmation step.
Review.Expectation: Success toast: 'verificationMode → dedicated-sessions. Kickoff prompt copied — paste it to your AI agent.' After refresh the row's fraction reads '2/2+'. The clipboard now holds the kickoff prompt. (Under the hood a verification_mode_change record was appended to the fixture copy's activity-log.json and the spec seed was aligned — the committed fixtures are untouched.)
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Live CLI run 2026-06-12 on a generated workspace copy: change_verification_mode exited 0 and appended the kind=verification_mode_change record; immediate re-run refused exit 3 (refused-already-dedicated). UI invocation/fallback branches are dependency-injection tested (setupVerification tests); writer gates have full Python coverage (S3).

Review.Subarea: Operator walk 10 — seed rewrite, both directions
Review.ItemLabel: On a NOT-STARTED Mode-A set the toggle rewrites the spec seed with confirmation, and B→A is still offered (no durable record yet)
Review.HumanAction: On 001-greet-the-world (not-started), open the row menu and pick 'Set Up Dedicated Verification…'. Read the confirmation copy, confirm A→B. Observe the row. Then run it again and switch back B→A.
Review.Expectation: Confirmation names the consequence (the session count can grow — N/M+). After A→B the row's fraction gains the '+'. The reverse direction is offered ONLY here (no activity-log history) and returns the row to its original quiet Mode-A state.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: verificationModeRewrite tests (byte-preserving matrix) + setupVerification tests (B→A hidden once ANY activity-log record exists — the S2 verifier Major, fixed and pinned).

Review.ItemLabel: 'Switch Tier…' (not-started only) rewrites the tier byte-preservingly; switching to Full fires the router-config guardrail toast
Review.HumanAction: On 001-greet-the-world (not-started), pick 'Switch Tier…' → 'Full'. Observe toasts and the row. Right-click 005-shout-the-greeting and 002-greet-quietly to confirm 'Switch Tier…' is absent there. Optionally switch 001 back to Lightweight.
Review.Expectation: 001 loses its 'lw' marker; an info toast confirms the switch; a separate WARNING toast notes ai_router/router-config.yaml was not found in this workspace (the fixture project has none). The action is absent on non-not-started rows. (A no-API-key warning appears only if no provider key is visible to VS Code — with keys set in your environment it should not fire.)
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: tierRewrite.test.ts (byte-preservation, guardrails, same-tier message, malformed repair) + actionRegistry.test.ts (not-started-only gating).

Review.ItemLabel: 'Migrate to v4 schema' on the v3 fixture clears the asterisk
Review.HumanAction: On hello-world-full/004-legacy-greeting-notes, open the row menu and pick 'Migrate to v4 schema'. Observe the notification and the row after refresh.
Review.Expectation: The migration succeeds (notification reports it; a session-state.v3.bak.json appears beside the state file in the fixture copy) and the asterisk disappears.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Live CLI run 2026-06-12 on a generated copy under a cp1252 console: exit 0, clean ASCII output. NOTE: this session FIXED a real defect here — the migrator banners printed Unicode arrows/em-dashes that crashed under Windows cp1252 (the exact encoding the extension's spawn pipes use), which would have failed this very click. Fixed ASCII-only per the repo convention; migrator suites re-run green (154 passed).

Review.ItemLabel: N/M+ renders only on Lightweight dedicated-sessions sets without a completed typed verification; drops to honest N/M after one; absent on Full and Mode A
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified. (Live confirmations of the visible states ride Operator walk 1: rows 004 '1/2+', 005 '2/2+', 006 plain '3/3', 001/002/003 no '+'.)
Review.Expectation: Matrix holds across before/during/after typed-session states.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: tierLegibility.test.ts predicate matrix + uatMatrixFixtures.test.ts row pinning; TS suite green at the tracked baseline 2026-06-12.
Review.Feedback: AI pre-verified 2026-06-12 (Set 062 S5). TS 864 passing / 2 tracked Set-026 baseline failures.

Review.ItemLabel: Blocked marker suppressed on complete/cancelled rows; 'Open Prerequisite Spec' opens the prerequisite's spec.md
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified. (Optional 10-second live check: right-click hello-world-full/002-style-the-greeting → 'Open Prerequisite Spec' opens 001-hello-page/spec.md.)
Review.Expectation: Terminal rows never show the chain glyph; the action targets the unsatisfied prerequisite's spec file.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: prerequisites.test.ts (terminal suppression) + actionRegistry.test.ts (hasUnsatisfiedPrereqs gating).

Review.ItemLabel: Same-tier selection messages 'already on tier'; malformed tier scalar is repaired on same-tier pick; rewrites are byte-identical outside the scalar; no-API-key guardrail warns
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified.
Review.Expectation: Per the 061 checklist rows these subsume: already-on-tier info toast; 'Repaired …'s malformed tier declaration' on typo'd scalars; byte-preservation; key/router-config warning toasts on switch-to-Full.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: tierRewrite.test.ts full matrix (present/absent/quoted/commented/CRLF/malformed/same-tier/guardrails).
Review.Feedback: AI pre-verified 2026-06-12 (Set 062 S5). The router-config guardrail is also walked live in Operator walk 11.

Review.ItemLabel: v?/v+ predicate matrix: Full never; cancelled never; Mode A only when complete+no-note+no-typed-session; Mode B only when work complete and verification owed/in-flight; suppressed on completed verification (verdict moves to fraction tooltip)
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified. (The six visible states ride Operator walk 1/3.)
Review.Expectation: Matrix holds for every tier × mode × ledger × note × row-state combination.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: verificationMarker.test.ts predicate matrix + uatMatrixFixtures.test.ts; verificationMarkerFor is a pure function shared by renderer and tests (tierLegibility.ts).

Review.ItemLabel: 'Verification Kickoff' only on LW Mode-B rows without completed verification; 'Set Up Dedicated Verification…' only on LW Mode-A not-started/complete rows (never in-progress/cancelled/Full); B→A never offered once any activity-log record exists
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified. (Absences are implicitly visible during the operator walk.)
Review.Expectation: Eligibility matrix holds; in-progress sets are excluded by design (no contention with a running session).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: actionRegistry.test.ts + setupVerification tests incl. the S2-verifier-driven inspectActivityLog fail-loud gate and the S3 branch-matrix invocation/fallback tests.

Review.ItemLabel: change_verification_mode fail-loud gates (A→B only; Lightweight only; refuses unreadable activity log, typed sessions, in-flight work) and last-record-wins read path honored by start_session/close gates
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified.
Review.Expectation: All gates refuse with honest reasons (exit 2/3); read_verification_mode honors both record kinds, last valid wins; the stale-seed re-capture hazard (S3 audit F3) cannot revert a change record.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Python suite 1216 passed / 1 skipped 2026-06-12, incl. the S3 gate matrix and test_explicit_cli_b_to_a_cannot_supersede_change_record; live refusal run today exited 3 with code=refused-already-dedicated.

Review.ItemLabel: Missing python / ai_router import failure / writer refusal: the UI informs and changes NOTHING (no drift by construction)
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified.
Review.Expectation: On any writer failure the spec seed is not rewritten, no prompt is copied, and an actionable message points at the workspace .venv / dabblerSessionSets.pythonPath.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: setupVerification.ts dependency-injected tests cover spawn-failure, import-failure (describeAiRouterImportFailure), refusal envelopes, and the success-only seed-align ordering.

Review.ItemLabel: make-uat-workspace produces a disposable copy outside the repo that derives identically to the committed matrix, and pins dabblerSessionSets.pythonPath to the repo .venv (generated copy only)
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified. (You exercise the result throughout the walk.)
Review.Expectation: Generated copy derives identically through readSessionSets; the committed .code-workspace never carries a machine-specific path; python-backed row actions work in the generated workspace with zero setup.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: uatMatrixFixtures.test.ts generator suite incl. the new pythonPath-pinning test (Set 062 S5); live generation today printed the pinned interpreter and the writer/migrator CLIs ran against the copy successfully.
Review.Feedback: AI pre-verified 2026-06-12 (Set 062 S5). The pythonPath pinning is an S5 addition (flagged for the session verifier).

Review.ItemLabel: Migrator/check_migrations CLI output is cp1252-safe (ASCII-only glyphs)
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified.
Review.Expectation: v3→v4, bulk, and lightweight migrators plus check_migrations print ASCII-only ('->', '-'); no UnicodeEncodeError under a Windows cp1252 console (the encoding the extension's spawn pipes inherit).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Defect found and fixed this session (the v3→v4 banner crashed cp1252 AFTER applying the in-place write — exit 1 with the migration silently done). Post-fix live run exits 0 under cp1252; migrator test modules re-run: 154 passed.
Review.Feedback: AI pre-verified 2026-06-12 (Set 062 S5). Fix rides ai_router 0.17.0.

Review.ItemLabel: No-folder window shows the Open Folder CTA; commands register before any early return
Review.HumanAction: Optional 30-second spot-check: open a VS Code window with no folder and view the Dabbler side panel.
Review.Expectation: The Open Folder call-to-action view renders; no hang.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: activationNoFolder.test.ts drives the real activate() with no workspaceFolder (the Set 059 regression pin). Sets 061/062 did not touch the activation-order or CTA code paths; the surface was operator-UAT'd on 0.29.0 (Set 060).
Review.Feedback: AI pre-verified 2026-06-12 (Set 062 S5) — mechanical pin + unchanged surface since the 0.29.0 operator UAT. Walk it live only if you want the extra assurance.

Review.ItemLabel: A workspace with no session sets shows the Getting Started form (tier radio, parallel checkbox, staged buttons)
Review.HumanAction: Optional 30-second spot-check: open any empty folder and view the panel.
Review.Expectation: The staged Getting Started form renders as shipped in 0.29.0; creating a set replaces it with the Explorer.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Getting Started rendering/staging tests from Set 060; git diff vsix-v0.29.0..HEAD confirms the only Getting-Started-adjacent changes are behavior-neutral (promptTier exported with an optional placeholder whose wizard default is unchanged; two new command registrations appended after the existing ones in activate()); operator-UAT'd on 0.29.0.
Review.Feedback: AI pre-verified 2026-06-12 (Set 062 S5) — unchanged surface since the 0.29.0 operator UAT.
